metadata.xai_summary: GradCAM++ for 'BCC': focus on central region. 4.0% pixels >70% activation. Peak=1.000. Strong spatial evidence.

clinical_report.findings: TRACE ConvNeXt-Base predicts BCC � Basal Cell Carcinoma - most common skin cancer, locally invasive, rarely metastatic. � with high confidence (89.4%).
clinical_report.morphological_description: Typically presents as a pearly or translucent nodule with rolled edges and telangiectasia. May show ulceration in advanced cases. Slow-growing, locally destructive.
clinical_report.differential_diagnosis:
BCC (89.4%): Basal Cell Carcinoma - most common skin cancer, locally invasive, rarely metastatic.
  BKL (6.5%): Benign Keratosis-like Lesion - seborrhoeic keratosis or solar lentigo, benign.
  MEL (2.5%): Melanoma - most dangerous skin cancer, high metastatic potential, urgent referral.
clinical_report.xai_interpretation: GradCAM++ highlights central region (4.0% pixels >70% activation, peak=1.00). Focused lesion-localised activation � high spatial interpretability confidence.
clinical_report.recommended_next_step: Refer to dermatology within 2 weeks for biopsy and excision planning. Surgical excision with 4 mm margins is first-line treatment. Mohs micrographic surgery for high-risk locations (face, ears).
clinical_report.disclaimer: AI research report only. NOT a clinical diagnosis. Must be reviewed and confirmed by a qualified dermatologist.

metadata.xai_summary: GradCAM++ for 'BKL': focus on lower-left region. 2.5% pixels >70% activation. Peak=1.000. Strong spatial evidence.

clinical_report.findings: TRACE ConvNeXt-Base predicts BKL � Benign Keratosis-like Lesion - seborrhoeic keratosis or solar lentigo, benign. � with moderate confidence (73.8%).
clinical_report.morphological_description: Characterised by a waxy, stuck-on appearance with a rough surface. Colour varies from tan to dark brown. Well-demarcated borders. Multiple lesions common in older adults.
clinical_report.differential_diagnosis:
BKL (73.8%): Benign Keratosis-like Lesion - seborrhoeic keratosis or solar lentigo, benign.
  BCC (19.6%): Basal Cell Carcinoma - most common skin cancer, locally invasive, rarely metastatic.
  MEL (3.7%): Melanoma - most dangerous skin cancer, high metastatic potential, urgent referral.
clinical_report.xai_interpretation: GradCAM++ highlights lower-left region (2.5% pixels >70% activation, peak=1.00). Focused lesion-localised activation � high spatial interpretability confidence.
clinical_report.recommended_next_step: Reassure patient - benign lesion requiring no treatment unless cosmetically bothersome. Monitor for rapid morphological change. Cryotherapy or curettage available if desired. Annual skin check recommended.
clinical_report.disclaimer: AI research report only. NOT a clinical diagnosis. Must be reviewed and confirmed by a qualified dermatologist.
